commit.message:
refactor: migrate figure serving to Supabase Storage

Figures are generated by the scientific pipeline and should NOT be
committed to git. This commit:

- Removes all 467 tracked binary figures from the git index
  (files remain on disk but are no longer tracked)
- Adds figures/**/*.png|jpg|svg|pdf to .gitignore to prevent
  future accidental commits
- Implements Supabase Storage support in figureUrl() utility:
 returns Supabase
      public URL (production mode, no files in git needed)
 falls back to /api/figures route (local dev mode,
      reads from filesystem)
- Adds scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py to sync generated
  figures from local disk to Supabase Storage bucket 'figures'
- Updates web/.env.example with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL
- Rewrites web/DEPLOYMENT.md with full Supabase Storage workflow

To restore figures in production:
  1. Create 'figures' bucket (public) in Supabase Storage
  2. Run: python scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py
  3. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL in Vercel env vars

Local dev continues to work without Supabase: figures served from
disk via /api/figures as long as they exist locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: commit figures so Vercel can serve them via /api/figures route

Previously all figures/**/*.png were gitignored, so Vercel deployed
the site without any figure files on disk, causing all <Image> elements
to show broken icons.

Changes:
- Remove figures/**/*.png|jpg|pdf|svg from .gitignore
- Keep ignoring large data files (*.nc, *.csv, *.parquet)
- Commit all existing figures in figures/cluster/, figures/main/,
  figures/exploratory/, figures/ep1_full/, figures/ep1_vertical/
- Also commit updated .gitignore exception for figures/ep_structure/

The /api/figures route reads files relative to the repo root via
process.cwd()/.., which works on Vercel because the full repo is
checked out and web/ is the Root Directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

synchronously before React attaches the onLoad handler, leaving

img.complete after mount to catch already-loaded images.

Add battery-backed SRAM support (#215)

* Initial plan

* Add battery-backed SRAM support (iNES header flag, MSBuild property, NESLib constants)

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt alphabetical ordering for SRAM constants

Move SRAM_END and SRAM_START entries to their correct alphabetical
position (after PPU_STATUS, before VRAMBUF_VERT) instead of between
MMC3_BANK_SELECT and MMC3_IRQ_DISABLE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add battery sample screenshot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: assert only battery bit in Write_BatteryFlag

Change Write_BatteryFlag to mask flags6 with 0x02 and assert only the
battery bit, not the entire byte. This makes the test resilient to
changes in other flags6 bits (mirroring, mapper nibble).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix battery sample: avoid transpiler bugs with rem/shr/local arrays

The original battery sample used patterns the transpiler doesn't handle
correctly in user functions:
- byte[] local arrays passed to vram_write (wrong pointer/length)
- value % 10 rem opcode (infinite loop when value=0)
- value >> 4 result stored in TEMP gets lost
- NTADR_A() inside user functions uses wrong arguments
- local + constant addition drops the local variable

Rewritten display_count to use only working patterns:
- vram_adr with hardcoded 0x2189 (not NTADR_A in function)
- Inline (value & 0x0F) + 0x30 expression with vram_put
- No local byte arrays, no rem, no shr

Updated test DLLs and verified.bin to match new output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove stray test script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix calorie stats: only count days with meals logged

The regression window can start well before the first meal was logged.
Previously every day in the window (including untracked days) was counted
as 0 kcal, dragging avg down and inflating std dev. Now only days with
at least one MealLog entry are included.

Verified against copy DB: 22 logged days, avg 3220 ± 768 kcal/day.
